shell.unarchiveTitle: Restore this project to the active list
shell.discardMessage: You have unsaved edits on the current page. Switching projects will discard them.

home.subtitle: Five stages from raw submissions to a finished report. Jump into any stage below, or run the whole pipeline end to end.
home.helpHint: New here? Work left to right — each stage hands off to the next.
home.helpBody1: This is your end-to-end pipeline. Start at Extract to connect a form, then move through the stages in order: clean and check your data, combine it, build charts and indicators, then deliver a Word report. You can run the whole thing at once, or open one stage at a time.
home.helpBody2: Each stage below has its own ? Help button with the same kind of guidance, so you are never far from an explanation.
home.firstRunLead: New here? Start by connecting your form — the rest unlocks once your data is in.

extract.label: Extract
extract.desc: Connect a Kobo, Ona, or INFORM form and configure the AI provider.

transform.label: Clean & check
transform.desc: Tidy and rename your questions, hide fields you don’t need, then check the data for problems before you use it.

model.label: Combine data
model.desc: Link related answers together and group them into combined tables (for example, totals per region) that your charts and reports can reuse.

analyze.label: Analyze
analyze.desc: Build charts, indicators, summaries, and the results framework — or just ask a question of your data.

present.label: Deliver
present.desc: Export data to files or a database, set report output options, then generate and manage Word reports.

sources.setupSub: Choose a platform, point at the right form, and pick the voice the AI uses when it writes narrative blocks.
sources.outputSub: Pick the export formats and destinations, decide where reports are written, and set the active period.
sources.outputHelpHint: Choose where your data and reports are written before you run.
sources.outputHelpBody1: Pick one or more export formats (CSV, Excel, JSON, or a database) and where the files should land. Set the active reporting period here too, so charts and reports cover the right dates.
sources.outputHelpBody2: You don’t have to fill in everything — just the destinations you actually use. The reference covers each format and the matching settings.
sources.setupHelpHint: Connect your form first — paste the server URL, token, and form ID.
sources.setupHelpBody1: This stage points the tool at your survey. Choose your platform (Kobo, Ona, or INFORM), paste the server URL and your API token, then enter the form’s ID. Once connected, the next stages can pull your questions and data.
sources.setupHelpBody2: The AI configuration sub-tab is optional — it only affects the narrative text the tool can draft for you. You can skip it and add it later.
sources.platformHint: Switch between Kobo Toolbox and Ona / INFORM — fields adapt to match.
sources.apiBaseUrlHint: Token is the Django REST token — fetch from your profile.
sources.apiTokenHint: Stored encrypted at rest and hidden once saved. Use <c>env:VARNAME</c> to read it from an environment variable instead.
sources.aliasHint: Slug used for file outputs and the URL.
sources.pullHint: Fetch the form schema, then download its submissions.
sources.fetchTitle: Pull the latest form schema (preserves your renames + hidden/PII flags)
sources.fetchViewerTitle: Viewer access — fetching questions requires an editor or admin role
sources.downloadTitle: Download submissions for the configured questions (applies filters + PII gating)
sources.downloadViewerTitle: Viewer access — downloading data requires an editor or admin role
sources.noTokenHint: Load a small example dataset to explore the next stages — no API token or AI key needed.
sources.sampleTitle: Load a bundled example dataset so you can try the tool without credentials
sources.sampleViewerTitle: Viewer access — loading sample data requires an editor or admin role
sources.sampleLoaded: Sample data loaded — the next stages now have example questions and rows.
sources.sampleError: Could not load the sample data.
sources.providerHint: OpenAI-compatible providers (Gemini, OpenRouter, Groq, DeepSeek…) auto-fill the right Base URL.
sources.apiKeyHint: Hidden once saved. Use <c>env:VARNAME</c> to read from an environment variable.
sources.baseUrlHint: Auto-filled for {{provider}}. Edit only if your endpoint differs.
sources.aiConnHint: AI buttons stay locked until this passes. Save your changes, then test.
sources.aiProbeTitle: Send a tiny probe to verify the provider + key work
sources.aiSetFirst: Set a provider, model and API key first
sources.outputCardSub: Where exports are written and what formats are emitted.
sources.formatsHint: Sets the format of the exported data files shown on the Reports tab. CSV and XLSX ship today; JSON and database exports are on the roadmap.
sources.dbConnectionHint: Written to <c1></c1>. Use the <c2></c2> prefix to read secrets from environment variables.
sources.ifExistsFail: fail — error if the table exists
sources.splitByHint: Generate one report per unique value of a categorical question.
sources.splitWillProduce: Will produce 1 report per unique value.
sources.exportNameHint: Base name for generated reports. Period and split suffixes are appended automatically.
sources.reportingPeriodHint: Reports include only submissions whose date falls in this window.
sources.periodNone: No period set — reports include all submissions.
sources.periodPending: · pending: {{label}} ({{started}} → {{ended}}) — click Set period, then Save.
sources.tip1: Prefix the token with <c1></c1> (e.g. <c2></c2>) to read it from an environment variable instead of saving the literal value.
sources.tip2: Run Test connection before fetching questions — it checks the API URL, token, and form UID.
sources.tip3: Re-fetching questions preserves your edits: renamed columns and adjusted categories carry over, and only new fields are added.

questions.title: Rename what shows up
questions.sub: Each row is a survey question. Edit the report column name to change how that column appears in your charts, indicators, and Word report — no YAML required.
questions.colReportNameSub: This is the name shown in your report, spreadsheet, and charts.
questions.piiBadgeTitle: Flagged as PII — excluded from AI metadata
questions.dupInputTitle: Duplicate export label — rename so it is unique
questions.flagPiiTitle: Flag as PII — exclude from AI metadata
questions.emptyPost: to pull the schema from your platform.
questions.viewerEditTitle: You have viewer access — editing questions requires an editor or admin role
questions.helpHint: Edit the report column name to change how a question appears everywhere.
questions.helpBody1: Every row here is one survey question. The most useful thing you can do is rename the report column so it reads cleanly in your charts and Word report — no YAML needed.
questions.helpBody2: You can also hide fields you don’t need, flag personal data (PII), and set each question’s category so the right chart types are offered later. Use Fetch questions to pull the latest form schema; your edits are kept.
questions.refetchTitle: Re-fetch the form schema (preserves your edits)
questions.autoHideTitle: Ask the AI to flag non-analytical fields
questions.flagPiiAiTitle: Ask the AI to flag personal-data fields
questions.dupStillShare: {{count}} fields still share a duplicate export label. Accept a suggested name (Use / Accept all proposals) or rename each manually, then Save changes.
questions.dupStillShare_one: {{count}} field still shares a duplicate export label. Accept a suggested name (Use / Accept all proposals) or rename each manually, then Save changes.
questions.dupResolved: All duplicates resolved — click Save changes to continue.
questions.appliedNames: Applied suggested names — review and Save changes

composition.viewsSub: Link related answers together and group them into combined tables (for example, totals per region) that your charts, summaries, and indicators can reuse.
composition.composeSubLead: Define what shows up in the report — charts, indicators, and summaries. Add manually, or let AI propose a set from your
composition.suggestKindTitle: Let AI propose {{kind}} from your questions
composition.starterSub: The quickest way in: ask a question in plain language, or let us draft a starter set of charts from your questions. You can refine everything below.
composition.viewsHelpHint: Group related answers into reusable tables — like totals per region.
composition.viewsHelpBody1: A view is a saved, combined table built from your data — for example, the number of responses per region, or an average score per site. Once you define a view, your charts, indicators, and summaries can all reuse it.
composition.viewsHelpBody2: This is optional. If your charts work straight off your questions, you can skip views entirely.
composition.composeHelpHint: Add charts, indicators, and summaries — or let AI propose a starter set.
composition.composeHelpBody1: This is where you decide what appears in the report. Add a chart to visualise answers, an indicator to track a single number, or a summary for AI-written narrative. Each one becomes a placeholder your Word template fills in.
composition.composeHelpBody2: Not sure where to start? Use the AI suggestion buttons to propose a starter set from your questions, then keep what fits.
composition.placeholderCaveat: Chart and table placeholders embed images, so they only work inside a template made with <c>generate-template</c> — copying is for reference. Indicator and summary tokens are plain text and paste anywhere.
composition.summariesSubPost: placeholders. Composed by the AI narrative.
composition.viewsCardSub: Link related answers and group them into combined tables — for example, totals per region — that your charts, summaries, and indicators can reuse.
composition.templatesSub: The Word version that holds the composition. Drop tokens anywhere you'd just type text.
composition.noTemplates: No templates yet — run generate-template from the Dashboard or upload a .docx.
composition.tokenAnatomySub: Drop any of these tokens into the Word template — Databridge fills them in step 4.
composition.tipBody: Rename questions in the Questions step before you start composing — labels flow through to every chart, indicator, and summary automatically.
composition.frameworkSub: Goal → Outcomes → Outputs. Link indicators to nodes for logframe rendering.
composition.deleteNodeMessage: This node will be removed from the results framework.
composition.previewTip: Tip: previews need a downloaded data file in <c1></c1>. Run <c2></c2> first if you haven’t.
composition.dropColumnHint: Click <s>×</s> to drop a column · click the name to rename it.
composition.downloadCsvTitle: Download the full view table as CSV
composition.columnsHint: Pick from your questions — type to search; press Enter to add a custom name
composition.columnsHintDot: Pick from your questions — type to search; press Enter to add a custom name.
composition.compareToHint: Optional. Set 'baseline' to compute delta + percent change from the baseline period.
composition.frameworkLinkHint: Optional. Pick a goal/outcome/output node to link this indicator to.
composition.disaggregateHintPre: Optional. Computes the stat per group; adds
composition.primaryHint: Optional. Marks this as the framework node's headline indicator — drives the node's achievement % in the logframe.
composition.primaryIndicator: Primary indicator for its framework node
composition.describeViewSub: — let AI fill the form below
composition.describePlaceholder: e.g. total students per department, only villages with students
composition.aiNoView: AI did not return a view — try rephrasing.
composition.sourceTableHint: Which base table this view draws from
composition.keepColumnsHint: Which columns to keep — leave blank for all
composition.filterHint: pandas query syntax, e.g. Age > 18 and Region == 'North'
composition.rollUp: Roll rows up into group totals

reports.title: Browse
reports.sub: Word reports rendered by build-report appear here. Download individual files or grab everything as a zip.
reports.helpHint: Finished Word reports land here — download one or compare periods.
reports.helpBody1: Every time you build a report, the finished .docx file appears in this list. Download a single report, grab them all as a zip, or build a comparison across two periods to show change over time.
reports.helpBody2: If the list is empty, run build-report first — you need downloaded data and a template in place before a report can be generated.
reports.buildSubtitle: Render a Word report from the latest data
reports.comparePeriodsTitle: Build a comparison report across periods
reports.viewerDeleteTitle: Viewer access — deleting requires an editor or admin role
reports.noReportsYet: No reports yet — run build-report from the Dashboard.
reports.noDataFilesYet: No data files yet. Run download first.
reports.buildReadyTitle: Render a Word report from the latest data
reports.pickPeriods: Pick 2 or more periods to compare:
reports.noPeriods: No periods configured yet. Add some in the Sources tab.
reports.deleteReportMessage: “{{name}}” will be permanently deleted. This can’t be undone.
reports.deleteAllMessage: Every generated .docx report will be permanently deleted. This can’t be undone.
reports.deleteSessionMessage: All files from session {{sid}} will be permanently deleted. This can’t be undone.

templates.title: Word
templates.subPre: Manage the .docx templates used by build-report. Each placeholder (
templates.helpHint: A template is your Word layout with placeholders the report fills in.
templates.helpBody1: A template is a normal Word .docx file with placeholders like or . When you build a report, each placeholder is swapped for the real chart, number, or text.
templates.helpBody2: The safest path is to let the tool auto-generate a template from your charts — chart placeholders must be unbroken, so typing them by hand often breaks. Upload your own only if you know the layout is sound.
templates.gateHint: Download data and configure questions before using Express fill.
templates.bannerText: In a hurry? Upload a template and let AI fill it
templates.readingTemplate: Reading the template and inferring specs…
templates.resolveFlagged: Resolve or drop the flagged row(s) to continue.
templates.reportBuilding: The report is building — see the <s>Reports</s> tab when it finishes.
templates.uploadAdminRequired: Admin access required to upload templates
templates.noTemplates: No templates yet — run generate-template from the Dashboard or upload a .docx.
templates.deleteAdminRequired: Admin access required to delete templates
templates.deleteMessage: “{{name}}” will be permanently deleted. This can’t be undone.

buildOptions.title: Build options
buildOptions.hint: Split one report per value of a main-table column, and preview a few groups before running the full split build.
buildOptions.splitPlaceholder: No split — type to filter columns
buildOptions.groupsHint: Choose a split-by column to preview groups
buildOptions.previewNote: Preview only — builds the first {{n}} group(s) so you can check the layout before committing to the full split build.
